def: v.n. = اڙنا uṛnā, q.v.
s.f. Shine, light, lustre, radiance, splendour; rising (of the moon, &c.).
= ادت udit, q.v.
s.m. Otter; crab; any aquatic animal.
wety: From Sanskrit.

def: = H ادڙگدڙ उदड़ गुदड़ udaṛ-gudaṛ, گدڙ or گدڙا redupl.), s.m. rags and tatters; old rags; cast-off clothes.
s.m. A wooden mortar used for pounding rice and separating the husk ( = okhlī, q.v.); — a gum resin, bdellium.

ety: inf. n. of عدل 'to decline, deviate, turn aside or away'
def: s.m. Declining, swerving, receding, returning; refusing; deserting; disobedience (of orders), insubordination
subs: s.m. One who is disobedient or refractory
s.f. Disobedience of orders, refractoriness, insubordination; evasion of a sentence or process
v.n. To disobey orders, to refuse to obey, to be refractory
(-se), To deviate (from), turn aside or away (from), to rebel (against), to disobey; to decline, refuse.

def: s.m. pl. (of ʻādil, q.v.), Just men, &c.
s.m. The glomerous fig-tree (Ficus glomerata) or gūlar tree.
s.m. Raft, float; the moon.
s.m. Marriage, wedding.

s.m. Perturbation, anxiety, trouble, care, solicitude, perplexity, agitation, uneasiness; bewilderment; fear, apprehension, consternation; exertion; haste, hurry, impetuosity; — the fruit of the Areca catechu, betel nut.
adj. Perturbed, troubled, perplexed, distressed, anxious, agitated; bewildered; hasty, precipitate.
adj. Sorrowful, anxious, perplexed, troubled; dejected.
s.m. Effort, exertion, endeavour; labour, toil, pains, trouble; employment, calling, business, trade
subs: To use effort or exertion, to exert oneself, to endeavour, take pains or trouble; to labour, toil; to follow a trade or calling.

def: adj. Active, energetic, laborious, persevering; prompt; engaged in trade, &c.
s.m. Royal pleasure-ground, park or garden; — motive, purpose.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic أَدْيَان (ʔadyān).

def: s.m. Bringing to a conclusion; closing ceremony; dedication of a temple, &c.
part. adj. Raised, held up; active, persevering, assiduous, diligent, laborious; eager, prepared, ready; — s.m. Section, chapter, division (of a book)
s.m. Effort, exertion, continued endeavour, perseverance; active preparation; plan, scheme
subs: To exert oneself, strenuously endeavour; to seek diligently after; to contrive.

def: adj. Active, energetic, laborious, persevering.
s.m. Light, lustre.

intj. (expressive of anxiety, distress of mind, vexation, disgust, &c.), Oh! oh dear! alas! ugh! foh! faugh! fye! pish! pooh! — s.m. Dirt, filth, rubbish; a mere puff or blast of breath, &c. (in this sense the word does not occur alone, but in combination with the verbs kar-denā and ho-jānā; see below)
subs: v.n. To utter the exclamation uff by reason of disquietude, distress, &c.; to heave a sigh, to sigh, lament; to signify disapproval or contempt; to pooh-pooh (at), think lightly (of)
v.t. To reduce to dirt or dust, to ruin, destroy, reduce to nothing

uff na nikālnā, v.n. To make no sign indicative of distress, vexation, &c., not to breathe a syllable, to endure in silence; to make no sign of sympathy or compassion, to shew perfect indifference to distress, &c.
v.n. To be reduced to dirt or dust, to become ruined, destroyed, &c.

def: s.m. Horizon; a tract or region of the earth; (poet.) the world.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian افق (ufuq), from Arabic أُفُق (ʔufuq).

def: part. = افتاده uftāda, q.v.; — (as a v.n.) s.f. State of helplessness; — beginning of life, childhood.

ety: perf. part. of uftādan; Z. aibi+rt. pat; S. abhi+pat
def: part. adj. Fallen, lying flat or horizontally; lying waste or untilled (land); poor, wretched, helpless
adv. Falling and getting up with great difficulty, &c. ( = girte-paṛte, q.v.).
s.f. Fall, act of falling; helplessness, weakness.

ety: for A. عفونه, inf. n. of عفن 'to become putrid,' &c.
def: s.f. Putridity, rottenness, corruption; stinking, stink, fetidness; effluvia; infection.

ety: for A. عفوصة, fr. ʻafṣ, q.v.
def: s.f. Astringency; constipation; bitterness.
s.f. Collections, proceeds, produce, collection of rent or revenue; sum of money realized or paid by instalments; payment by instalments; dues, money to be realized; usury.
v.t. To gather, collect, accumulate; to realize, raise (money or funds), net; to tax, levy, impose; to beg (for), to importune (also written ughānā).
s.m. That which is spit out after chewing anything (especially betel-leaf)
subs: s.m. (or ugāl-dānī, s.f.), A vessel for spitting in, a spittoon (syn. pīkdān).

def: s.m. A certain insect which attacks standing crops; land always saturated with moisture; ooze, slime.
v.t. To spit out; — v.n. To chew the cud, to ruminate ( = jugālnā).

v.t. To spit out; to bring up from the stomach or craw (as birds for their young, or as cattle in chewing the cud); to throw up, vomit, reject, disgorge; to restore or refund (property surreptitiously obtained = ugālnā, q.v.)
subs: v.n. To be spit out, &c.; to fall out of its sheath (a sword, &c.).

def: v.t. To cause to grow, to raise.
v.t. To clean (a well) by removing the sediment of black mud, &c.
s.f. Growing, springing up; production; invention; inventive power or genius ( = ukat).
v.t. To abuse, revile, reproach, use bad language to.
s.f. = ugāhī, q.v.
v.t. = ugāhnā, q.v.
adj. (f. -ī), Uncovered, unveiled, laid bare, exposed; disclosed, discovered; bare, naked, undressed; plain, clear, evident, manifest. (See ughṛā).

ety: ughāṛ˚ = Prk. उग्घाडे(इ) or उग्घाड(इ) = S. उद्धाटय(ति), rt. उद्+घट्
def: v.t. To uncover, unveil, unclose, open, lay bare, expose; to pull off, throw off (covering or dress); to disclose, reveal; to betray (a secret or confidence)
v.n. To be uncovered, unveiled, laid bare, opened, exposed, disclosed, discovered; to be pulled off.
s.m. One who discovers or unveils, &c. (see ughāṛnā).
v.n. To be collected, gathered, raised (by subscription), &c. (See ugāhnā.)
(part. of next, q.v.) = ughāṛā, q.v.
v.n. To grow, springup, shoot, sprout, germinate; to be produced, to rise, bud; to begin, set in; to dawn
subs: To be withered or blasted at its birth, to be nipped in the bud.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀉𑀕𑁆𑀕𑁂𑀇 (uggei), from Sanskrit *उद्गाति (*udgāti). Cognate with Gujarati ઉગવું (ugvũ), Punjabi اُگّنا (uggnā) / ਉੱਗਣਾ (uggaṇā), Sindhi اُڳڻ (ug̠ṇ) / उॻणु (ug̠aṇu).

def: s.m. Anger, wrath; — adj. Fierce, wrathful, terrible, cruel, savage; an epithet of Śiva; the name of a mixed tribe, the offspring of a Kshatriya by a Sudra woman (the employment of this tribe is to kill animals that live in holes); powerful, violent, mighty, strong, formidable; sharp, severe
ex: اَگَرْ مَیں نے یَہ کِیَا ہوتَا تو ...
agar ma͠i-ne ye kiyā hotā to
If I had done this, then ...
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit अगरु (agaru).

def: s.m. Cessation of an eclipse, illumination of the sun or moon after an eclipse.
s.m. Cessation of an eclipse, illumination of the sun or moon after an eclipse.
s.m. Growing, growth; becoming.
intj. Oh! hah!
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit इह (ihá), doublet of یَہاں (yahā̃).

def: s.m. Deliberation; reasoning, logic, inference; supplying an ellipsis.
s.m. Deliberation; reasoning, logic, inference; supplying an ellipsis.
adj. That = wǒh, q.v.
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit इह (ihá), doublet of یَہاں (yahā̃).

def: adv. There = wahāṅ, q.v.

ety: S. अप+हर्त्ता or हार, rt. हृ
def: s.m. Covering, wrapper; cover or curtain of a cart, litter, pālkī, &c. (as a protection from sun or rain, or as a means of concealment for women: — see ohār and uhāṛ).
s.m. Cover, wrapper, &c. = uhār, ohār, q.q.v.
v.t. To uncover, make bare, &c. = ughāṛnā, q.v.
v.n. To be laid bare, be uncovered, be naked; to subside (as a swelling or inundation); to settle (as a liquid, &c.).
pron. emphat. That very, &c. = uhi, wǒhī, q.v.
adj. Inferrible, to be supplied (in reasoning); elliptical; to be investigated.
intj. Expressing pleasure, pain, fear, surprise, &c. (used by women).

adj. Very apathetic, very cold, hard or unfeeling; very senseless, stupid or ignorant, intensely obtuse, idiotic; ill-mannered, unmannerly, uncouth, awkward, loutish, clownish, rude, rough, vulgar, coarse
subs: cold or unfeeling person; a very stupid fellow, an arrant blockhead, a fool; a clumsy, awkward fellow, a lout, an ill-mannered fellow, &c.

def: adj. Light; bright, shining; splendid, gaudy; celebrated, famous; conspicuous, manifest; — s.m. Light, brightness, splendour.
s.m. Light, day-light, day-break, day; sunshine; splendour, brightness, refulgence; glory, pride, ornament; — adj. (f. -ī), Light, clear, bright, shining, luminous, &c.
subs: To become light; to be lighted, (hence) to kindle up, take fire
s.m. The morning watch, the watch which begins at dawn
s.m. The morning star, the planet Venus.

def: v.n. To be bright, be polished, to shine; to be or become clean.
v.t. To make bright; to light, light up, cause to shine; to brighten; to clean, polish, furbish; to make white
subs: v.t. To light up, rub up, brighten, polish; to cleanse, make white; to wash (prov.).
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit उज्ज्वलयति (ujjvalayati). Compare Sindhi اُجرَڻُ (ujraṇu) / उजिरणु, اُجارَڻُ (ujāraṇu) / उजारणु, Vaghri ઊજારણુ (ūjārṇu), Gujarati ઉજાળવું (ujāḷvũ), અજવાળવું (ajavāḷvũ), Marathi उजळणे (ujaḷṇe), Odia ଉଜଳିବା (ujaḷibā), ଉଜାଳିବା (ujāḷibā).

def: s.m. The direction, or part, of a river which is opposite to the course of the stream; up the stream, against the tide.
adj. Laid waste, depopulated, demolished, ruined, in ruin, waste, desolate, &c. = ujāṛ, q.v.

ety: v.n. of next; see also ujaṛnā
def: adj. Laid waste, devastated, demolished, ruined, spoiled; depopulated, deserted, abandoned; waste, desolate (also written اوجڙ ūjaṛ)
subs: demolition, destruction, ruin, desolation; a desolate or ruined place, a ruin (syn. wirāna), uninhabited place, waste, desert, wilderness
adj. & s.m.f. Ugly, ill-favoured, unsightly, deformed; — ugly or ill-favoured person (especially a woman)

def: v.t. To root up; to lay waste, devastate, destroy, break up, pull or take to pieces, pull down, demolish, raze, wipe out; to ruin, spoil, plunder, injure, harm; to drive out (the inhabitants); to depopulate
subs: (intens. of ujāṛnā), v.t. To destroy utterly, &c.

def: v.n. To be rooted up, utterly destroyed or ruined, demolished, razed to the ground; to be laid waste, ravaged, plundered, spoiled; to be depopulated; to be devoid of inhabitants; to be wasted, squandered, dissipated; to fall into ruin or decay, go to waste, become a waste or ruin; to be or become deserted, desolate, cheerless or gloomy; to be lost; to perish, die; to be ruined, defiled, violated, dishonoured (a maid): — ujaṛ-jānā = ujaṛnā.
adj. & s.m. Destroying, demolishing; plundering; ruining, wasting, &c., squandering, wasteful; — destroyer, spoiler, plunderer, &c.; spendthrift; prodigal.
com. gend. = اجاڙو ujāṛū, q.v.
v.t. To cause to be laid waste, &c.

ety: from عجب ʻujiba, perf. pass. of iv, or fr. اعجاب ěʻjāb, inf. n. of iv of عجب 'to wonder'
def: s.m. Self-admiration, self-conceit, vanity, pride, haughtiness.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian عَجَب (ajab), from Arabic عَجَب (ʕajab).

def: (also uṅjelā) = اجالا ujālā. q.v.
v.n. To raise oneself on tip-toe for the purpose of observing, to peep, spy. (Cf. ućaknā.)
v.n. To flow or be poured from one vessel into another

ety: caus. from S. उन्द् cf. ujwānā
def: v.t. To pour from one vessel into another, to pour out, to empty.
adj. = ūjaṛ, q.v.
adj. Ignorant, stupid, clownish, &c. ( = اجڐ ujaḍ, q.v.).
v.t. = اجهالنا ujhālnā, q.v.
v.t. = اجهالنا ujhālnā, q.v.

s.m. Ujain, a city (and a province), so called, formerly the capital of Vikramāditya; it is one of the seven sacred cities of the Hindūs, and the point whence their geographers take their first meridian.
adj. Bright, luminous, shining, radiant, splendid; enlightened; beautiful, lovely, excellent; pure, clean, clear; white; plain, unadorned ( = sāda); simple, guileless; blown, expanded
subs: s.m. A plain or simple man; a man dressed in clean, white clothes; a respectable man
clear intellect, luminous or enlightened man
adj. Of radiant or luminous mind, clear-minded; of enlightened mind
The variety of cowhage or cowitch (Dolichos pruriens) with a white stem
The long white pumpion or pumpkin, Lagenaria vulgaris
ujlī mirćī, White pepper, Piper album
v.t. To clear one's honour or reputation; to exculpate; to acquit
v.n. To have one's honour reëstablished; to be exculpated; be acquitted
The viscid cleome (Cleome viscosa), the seeds of which, called ćhorī ajwān, are regarded by the native practitioners as anthelmintic and carminative
To be light; to be bright, be polished, to shine; to be or become clean; to be dressed in clean clothes.
wety: From Classical Persian اَجَل (ajal), from Arabic أَجَل (ʔajal).

def: part. adj. (f. -ā), Bright, clear, pure, clean. (See ujlā.)
adj. (f. -ā) = اجل ujjal, q.v.
s.m. Burning, blazing; shining forth.
s.f. = اجلاپن ujlāpan, q.v.
part. adj. (f. -ā) = اجلت ujjalit, q.v.
adj. Bright, luminous, shining, radiant, splendid; enlightened; beautiful, lovely, excellent; pure, clean, clear; white; plain, unadorned ( = sāda); simple, guileless; blown, expanded
subs: s.m. A plain or simple man; a man dressed in clean, white clothes; a respectable man
clear intellect, luminous or enlightened man
adj. Of radiant or luminous mind, clear-minded; of enlightened mind
The variety of cowhage or cowitch (Dolichos pruriens) with a white stem
The long white pumpion or pumpkin, Lagenaria vulgaris
ujlī mirćī, White pepper, Piper album
v.t. To clear one's honour or reputation; to exculpate; to acquit
v.n. To have one's honour reëstablished; to be exculpated; be acquitted
The viscid cleome (Cleome viscosa), the seeds of which, called ćhorī ajwān, are regarded by the native practitioners as anthelmintic and carminative
To be light; to be bright, be polished, to shine; to be or become clean; to be dressed in clean clothes.

def: s.f. Brightness; cleanness; whiteness, &c. ( = ujlāpan, q.v.); price paid for cleaning, &c.
v.t. = اجالنا ujālnā, q.v.
s.m. Brightness, radiance, luminosity; enlightenment; clearness; whiteness; plainness (e.g. us-kā ujlā-pan-hī gahnā thā).
s.m. Brightness, radiance, luminosity; enlightenment; clearness; whiteness; plainness (e.g. us-kā ujlā-pan-hī gahnā thā).
incor. for the next, q.v.
adj. Deserted, desolate, cheerless, gloomy, &c. ( = ujāṛ, q.v.)
subs: adj. In ruins, ruined, razed to the ground, desolate.

def: s.f. = اجر ajr, q.v.
wety: From Arabic أُجْرَة (ʔujra).

r: ʻūju ʼbnu ʻūq yā 'unuq, lit., met.
def: Og, king of Bashan (lit. 'Uj, the son of 'Uq or ʻUnuq); (met.) a tall foolish man.
s.m. A wonderful thing, wonder, miracle, prodigy.

ety: prob. for A. اعجوبة uʻjūbat, v.n. fr. عجب; see ʻajab
def: adj. & s.m. Wonderful ( = ʻajība), a wonderful thing, a wonder; a miracle.

ety: S. उन्दय˚, caus. of उद् 'to flow'
def: v.t. To cause a person to pour (water, &c.) from one vessel into another; to cause to be emptied; to empty (a vessel, &c. cf. ujhālnā); ujwā-lenā v.t. = ujwanā.
s.m. = اجالا ujālā, q.v.
s.m. = اجالا ujālā, q.v.
s.f. Omission, slip, mistake, &c. = ćūk, q.v.
(v.n. fr. عقب 'to follow after,' &c.), s.m. & f. An eagle.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian عُقَاب (uqāb), borrowed from Arabic عُقَاب (ʕuqāb).

def: s.m. pl. (of ʻāqil, q.v.), The wise, the prudent.
v.n. To boil up or over; to be boiling, to boil (cf. uklānā).
v.t. To boil (water, &c.): cf. uklānā.
s.m. The letter उ u
subs: adj. Ending in the letter ukār (उ u).

def: gen. of وه wǒh.
v.n. To be raised; to rise, mount, start up (in, -meṅ); to try to move, struggle to rise, to struggle; to be excited, moved, stirred, roused, &c.
part. adj. Spoken, said, aforesaid, above-mentioned, the said (used in law documents).

s.f. Speaking, speech, voice, language; story, fabrication, invention, contrivance; inventive power, originality, genius (in these last senses the word is probably derived from the S. udgati; see ugat)
subs: To make up a story, to concoct, fabricate, invent, contrive
To do or perform by means of one's inventive power, to invent, originate, create.

def: v.t. To dig up, turn up, cut up, root up; to unearth; to turn over, turn upside down, upset, turn topsy-turvy; to extract (a secret) gradually and artfully, worm out (a secret); to cast an obligation in one's teeth; to mar, ruin, destroy, spoil, plunder; to rob the good name of, to vilify, abuse; — v. int. To be dug up, turned, cut up, &c.: ukaṭ-ḍālna, v.t. (intens. of ukaṭnā, — generally used in the sense of), to tear, tear to pieces, root up, utterly ruin or destroy, &c.
v.t. To cause to be dug up, &c.
s.f. (prov.) The longing of a pregnant woman.
s.m. End, conclusion, termination; last state; accomplishment; consequence, result, issue; the future state, the next world (syn. ʻāqibat).

ety: for A. عقدة, v.n. fr. عقد 'to tie'
def: s.m. A knot, tie, bond ( = ʻaqd); a knob; a joint; a node; an excrescence; a gland; an impediment (in speech); an entanglement, a complication; entangled things, perplexed affairs, confused words; a knotty problem; a secret, mystery, enigma
subs: s.m. 'An opener of knots,' one who solves difficulties, &c.
s.f. The opening or untying of knots, the solving of difficulties.

def: v.t. To turn up, to undo, unpick, unravel, open out, rip up, &c.
s.f. (in H. also m.), Sugar-cane, Saccharum officinarum (in N.W. India generally called īkh)
subs: ūkh-bhoj, s.m. The day on which the planting of the sugar-cane begins (it is generally attended with some festive ceremonies)

def: s.m. Throwing up, vomiting; vomit; an emetic
s.f. A wooden or stone mortar for pounding rice or other grain.
s.f. A mortar to pound grain in = ukhli, q.v.
v.t. To throw up, vomit
s.m. Heat, &c., see ushma.
s.f. A pot or vessel for husking grain; — a small mortar = okhlī, q.v.
s.f. Rooting up, extirpation, eradication
s.f. A field of sugar-cane.
s.f. A field of sugar-cane.

ety: ukhāṛ˚ = Prk. उक्कड्ढ(इ), fr. उक्कड्ढ = S. उत्कृष्ट, p.p.p. of उद्+कृष्
def: v.t. To root up, eradicate, extirpate, pluck up or out, tear up or off, dig up; to break off, disjoin, sunder; to dislocate, displace; to take out, extract, draw out; to detach, separate, remove; to set (one) against, estrange, alienate; entice away, win over (from), buy off; to drive away, put away, do away with, dismiss; to upset, overturn, subvert; to scatter, disperse; to demolish, desolate, destroy, lay waste, devastate, exterminate

v.n. To be rooted up or out, be plucked up or out, be extracted, be dug up, be extirpated; to be wiped out, effaced; to be scattered, dispersed; to be pulled down, razed, demolished, &c.; to break up; to be struck (a tent); to slip out, be dislocated (a bone); to be pulled, torn or ripped off; to be broken or severed (as friendship); to become alienated or estranged; to become displeased or offended (with, -se); to be seared, frightened, driven away, &c. to be discouraged, disheartened, turned from, diverted, dissuaded, &c.; to make off, decamp, abscond, slink off, &c.; to be dismissed, sent adrift, &c.; to come undone, unravelled, unstitched, &c.; to come out, fall off or out (as the stone of a ring); to fall of, decline; to lose credit or reputation; to fail of (one's word); to sing or play out of tune; to be unsteady, shaky, straggling, irregular, inconsistent, &c. (see ukhṛā); to be cut, carved, or engraved (on, -par); to be stamped, impressed, &c.
subs: v.n. intens. of ukhaṛnā, and the more common form.

def: s.m. One who roots or digs up, eradicator, devastator, destroyer, &c.
v.t. To cause to be rooted out, to have (a thing) pulled out, &c.
v.n. To trip, stumble.
v.t. = اکهيڙنا ukheṛnā, q.v.
v.t. = اکهاڙنا ukhāṛnā, q.v.
subs: v.n. To be crusty or cross, to turn away the face (from), to be cool (towards).

def: v.t. = اکهڙوانا ukhaṛwānā, q.v.
s.f. = H اکهل उखल ukhal (contrac. of ūkhal),s.m. A wooden or stone mortar for pounding rice or other grain.
s.m. = ushma-ja, q.v.s.v. ushma.
adj. (f. -ī), Uncertain, indistinct, ill-formed, irregular, straggling, crooked, unsteady, inconsistent, bad; abrupt, sharp, harsh, caustic (speech)
subs: or ukhṛā-pukhṛā ḵẖat̤t̤, s.m. Straggling, irregular, crooked, &c. writing.

def: v.t. To root up, &c. = اکهاڙنا ukhāṛnā, and اکهڙوانا ukhaṛwānā.
s.f. Sister; female friend: — adj. Similar; congenial.
v.n. = اکتانا uktānā, q.v.
s.f. An ounce (weight); an ounce of silver, a silver coin weighing one ounce (value between six and seven shillings).
s.f. Sickness, nausea ( = ubkāʼī).

v.n. To be confounded, confused, agitated, excited, distracted; to feel anxious, uneasy, disquieted, restless; to tire (of, -se), weary (of), be disgusted (with), be sick (of); to feel nausea or sickness.
adj. Of or pertaining to the town of Uklesar (in Southern India, famous for its paper manufacture).
s.f. = اکهلی ukhlī, q.v.
= اوکنا uknā, q.v.

ety: ūk˚ or ok˚ = Prk. वमक्के(इ) or वमक्क(इ) = S. वम+कृ
def: v.n. To vomit (i.q. ubaknā, q.v.).
v.n. To fall short, fail (of, a duty, &c.), to miss, slip, blunder ( = ćūknā, q.v.)
subs: adv. By mistake, inadvertently, unwittingly; now and then, once in a way.

def: rustic for اسے use or اسکو usko, dat. of وه wǒh.
adj. Sitting on the hams with the soles of the feet on the ground, squatting
subs: v.n. To sit on one's hams, &c., to squat.

def: adj. Sitting on the hams with the soles of the feet on the ground, squatting
subs: v.n. To sit on one's hams, &c., to squat.

ety: S. उद्+कर्षणं, rt. कृष्; but cf. S. उत्सह्
def: v.t. To raise, lift; to trim (a lamp, by raising the wick); to kindle (a fire), fan (a flame), blow (fire), stir up (embers); to rouse, stir up, move, excite, stimulate, urge; to encourage, incite, instigate.
part. adj. Spoken, said, aforesaid, above-mentioned, the said (used in law documents).
s.m. One who casts an obligation in the teeth of; a low, shabby fellow.

v.n. To be sad, sorrowful, melancholy, dejected, &c.; to fret, be irritated (by, -se), chafe (at or under), be out of humour (with, -se); to be languid, be weary (of), be sick (of), to lose all relish (for), to be satiated or cloyed; to become irksome, wearisome, &c.
v.t. = اکٿنا ukaṭnā, q.v.
v.t. To stir up, rouse, instigate, incite; to support, aid, back, abet; to forward, advance, promote.
s.m. Instigator, inciter, aider, abettor; one who forwards or promotes, promoter.
s.f. = اکت ukat or ukti, q.v.

s.f. Speaking, speech, voice, language; story, fabrication, invention, contrivance; inventive power, originality, genius (in these last senses the word is probably derived from the S. udgati; see ugat)
subs: To make up a story, to concoct, fabricate, invent, contrive
To do or perform by means of one's inventive power, to invent, originate, create.

def: s.f. Punishment, chastisement, torture, torment.
s.f. pl. (of ʻaql), Intellects, understandings, minds; sciences.
adv. Conjecturally, at a guess, at a rough estimate or calculation.
adj. fem. of awwal, q.v.
v.t. = ulaćnā, q.v.
v.t. To throw out, draw off, pour off (water); to bale out; to drain ( = ulīćnā, q.v.).
v.t. = ulāngnā and ulangnā, q.q.v.
s.m. Twitting, casting in one's teeth; reproach; complaint, accusation ( = ulahnā)
subs: v.t. To twit, taunt, reproach; to accuse, complain against.

def: s.m. = ulāhnā, q.v.

ety: ulah˚ = Prk. उल्लह(इ) = S. उल्लभ(ते), rt. उद्+लभ्
def: v.n. To spring up, grow, vegetate; to reprove; to reproach, &c. (see ulāhnā; urěhnā).

ety: by metathesis for ujhalnā = S. उद+झट्: — Other forms are wrajhnā, arajhnā, arujhnā
def: v.n. To be entangled, ravelled, twisted, entwined; to be complicated, made intricate; to be perplexed; to be involved (in difficulties, &c.), to be at a loss; to fall foul (of, -se), dispute or wrangle (with); to interpose, interfere; to demand a reason (of, -se); to join in (with, -se) to cross-question; to be confused, confounded, disordered, deranged, jumbled; to be entrapped, ensnared, inveigled, enticed; to be captured, taken, shut up, confined, imprisoned; to be laid out, be invested (capital); to stick, be unable to proceed (in an affair, lesson, &c.); to have the heart set or fixed (on, -se), be enamoured (of), be captivated or charmed (with); to have a liaison (with), form an illicit connection (with); to be engaged (to), betrothed (to); to be married; to be busy, occupied, engaged (in, -meṅ), be intent (on); to carp or cavil (at, -par), find fault (with)
subs: (-se), To join in, close (with), fall foul (of), dispute (with), cavil (at); to interpose, interfere, &c. (see ulajhnā)
v.n. To be delayed or detained; to delay, tarry, linger, stick.

def: s.m. A kind of small boat.
adj. = ulāṛ, q.v.
v.t. = ulāṛnā, q.v.
v.n. = ulaṛnā, q.v.
s.m. pl. (of ʻālim), The learned; doctors of law and religion.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian عُلَمَاء (ulamā'), borrowed from Arabic عُلَمَاء (ʕulamāʔ), from عَالِم (ʕālim), from عَلِمَ (ʕalima).

def: s.m. Hard swelling; tumour; swollen gland.
v.t. = uleṇḍnā, q.v.
adj. Naked, nude.
v.t. To leap over or across, pass over or beyond, to cross; to bestride, mount, ride (a horse).
= ulangnā, q.v.
v.n. To leap, pass or go (over); — v.t. To leap over or across; to go beyond, overstep (see ulāngnā).
v.t. To leap over or across, pass over or beyond, to cross; to bestride, mount, ride (a horse).
adj. Fat, plump, in good condition.
adj. Liable to overturn, unstable; too heavily laden at the back (a cart), not having the burden equally disposed (a cart, &c.), top-heavy ( = ulāl).

ety: S. परा+वर्तनीयं, rt. वृत्; but cf. S. उल्लाल, rt. लल्
def: v.t. To overturn, upset, tilt-over, lay over to one side. (cf. ulaṭnā.)
v.t. To cause to lie down, to turn over on the side, to cause to sleep.
v.n. To lie down, to rest.
v.n. To topple over, fall over, roll over, tilt over, upset; to fall backwards, fall down, be thrown on the back; to lie over on one side; to careen (as a ship).
s.m. Leavings of food from the table of a great personage (of which the servants partake)
subs: v.t. To touch or taste (food); to take a morsel.

ety: S. परि or परा+वर्त rt. वृत्; cf. H. palaṭ
def: s.m. (f.?), Overturning, upsetting; reverting; inversion, reversal; inverse, reverse, opposite; contrary; the wrong side; return; subversion; change
subs: s.m. Confusion, jumble; rout and riot; interchange; deception, imposition, cheating; — adj. Higgledy-piggledy, topsy-turvy, &c. ( = ulṭā-pulṭā, q.v.)
v.t. To throw into confusion, to toss about, turn topsy-turvy, &c.
To be in a state of confusion, &c.
ulaṭ-peć (or peṅć), s.m. Perplexity, embarrassment; intricacy, maze, entanglement, difficulty; adversity, misfortune; turn or vicissitude of fortune; perversion; deception, trickery
and ulaṭ-peć-kā, adj. (f. -kī), Perplexing, intricate, labyrinthine; entangling, crooked, crafty (as conduct or speech)
s.m. Name of a certain plant and its flower, Abroma augustum.

def: v.n. To become proud, to have one's head turned.
v.t. & n. = ulaṭnā, q.v.
v.n. To undulate; to be agitated (as the ocean by storms).

ety: S. परा+वर्तनीयं, rt. वृत्, — cf. palaṭnā and lauṭnā
def: v.t. To overturn, upset, capsize, turn upside down, turn inside out, to invert, reverse; to subvert, overthrow, throw down, ruin, destroy; to throw (as a horse its rider); to turn over, prostrate, bring low, overcome, reduce to a state of helplessness or poverty; to throw up, vomit; to turn up, to dig; to change, pervert (meaning or sense); to recall, retract; to refute, rebut, contradict (anything said, e.g. bāt ulaṭnā); to pour (into); to pour out, empty; to toss or drink off; to send back, to return; to turn over (leaves of a book), go through (a book); to read over again; to revolve (in the mind); to transpose; to translate; to counteract; to act contrary to (orders, &c.), to disobey; to reverse (a decision); to cancel, rescind; — v.n. To be overturned, to be turned upside down, &c.; to topple over, to upset; to roll over, fall down; to change; to be changed, perverted, misrepresented, misconstrued; to be reversed or transferred (as a charm); to be dug up; to go or come back, to return; to turn (against, -se), revolt (from), rebel (against); to have (one's) head turned, to become proud; to be perturbed or agitated (the heart); to go mad; to be reduced to wretchedness or poverty, to be ruined, become bankrupt
subs: v.n. To fall down, fall over, &c. ( = ulaṭnā); to turn (upon or against, -par), to be opposed (to); to flock (to, -meṅ), go in crowds (to)
v.n. = ulaṭnā, and is the more common form
v.t. intensive of ulaṭnā, and is the more common form
adv. Over again, e.g. ulaṭ-ke kahnā, To say over again, to repeat.

def: v.t. To invert a vessel in order to empty it of its contents; to empty; to pour out (water, &c. = unḍelnā, q.v.).
s.m. (local), corr. of ʻulūfa, q.v.
s.f. Familiarity, intimacy; attachment, affection, friendship
subs: (-se), To be intimate (with); to cultivate friendship (with).

def: adj. Affectionate, attached, friendly, derived from friendship
subs: ( = ulfat-kā banda), A servant or slave attached from friendship (to his master, &c.).

def: v.t. To cause to leap over, cause to go beyond.
s.f. Leaping over (cf. ulgāṭ).
s.f. Leaping over. (ruśtic.)
acc. of, and = اولو ulū, q.v.
v.t. To throw up water, pour off water; to bale; to drain ( = ulaćnā, ulaćhnā, ujhalnā).
adj. Awkward, clumsy; stupid ( = angaṛh, q.v.); slovenly, untidy. (Used by women.)
adj. (f. -ī), Entangled, complicated, involved, intricate

s.f. = H الجهاو उलझाव uljhāʼo, s.m. = H الجهاوا उलझावा uljhāwā, s.m. Entanglement. complication, intricacy, involution; perplexity, anxiety, uneasiness; twist, ply, turn, windings and turnings, maze; doubling (as of a hare); confusion, disorder, derangement, disturbance; embroilment, imbroglio; difficulty, embarrassment; discord, misunderstanding, jar.

r: uljhānā, iljhānā, ulajhnā, urjhānā, irjhānā, arujhānā, arjhānā, ajhurānā
ety: caus. of ulajhnā, q.v. — Other forms are urjhānā, irjhānā, arujhānā, arjhānā, ajhurānā
def: v.t. To entangle, ravel (as thread); to involve, complicate, make intricate; to perplex, confound; to implicate; to entrap, insnare; to inveigle, entice, allure, beguile, mislead, deceive; to divert, entertain; to double (as a hare); to embroil, to involve in a quarrel; to make (one's wits) whirl round; to set (one's brain) to work; to throw into confusion, disarrange, jumble; to intertwine, interlace, to fasten (to, -se), connect (with), join (to); to unite by marriage; to put (one into, -meṅ, a berth); to shut up, commit to prison; to lay out, invest (capital); to put on (a garment, hastily or temporarily)
subs: v.t. To keep (one) diverted or beguiled, &c., to detain, delay
(and urjhānā-surjhānā), v.n. To be tossed about; to flutter, flap (as a flag).

def: s.m. = H الجهاوا उलझावा uljhāwā, s.m. = H الجهن उलझन uljhan, s.f. Entanglement. complication, intricacy, involution; perplexity, anxiety, uneasiness; twist, ply, turn, windings and turnings, maze; doubling (as of a hare); confusion, disorder, derangement, disturbance; embroilment, imbroglio; difficulty, embarrassment; discord, misunderstanding, jar.

s.m. = H الجهاو उलझाव uljhāʼo, s.m. = H الجهن उलझन uljhan, s.f. Entanglement. complication, intricacy, involution; perplexity, anxiety, uneasiness; twist, ply, turn, windings and turnings, maze; doubling (as of a hare); confusion, disorder, derangement, disturbance; embroilment, imbroglio; difficulty, embarrassment; discord, misunderstanding, jar.
s.m. = uljhāʼo, q.v. — (The form ilfheṛā also occurs. — Syn. bakheṛā, janjāl).
s.m. = uljhāʼo, q.v. — (The form ilfheṛā also occurs. — Syn. bakheṛā, janjāl).
s.f. (m.?) A firebrand; meteor; spark; flame; fire.
adj. Overstepping; transgressing; — s.m. One who oversteps; transgressor.
s.m. Leaping or passing over or beyond; overstepping; transgression, violation, infringement
subs: v.n. To be leaped or crossed over; to be transgressed or violated.

def: s.m. Jumping up, skipping; alacrity; gladness, joy, happiness; splendour, light; division of a book, chapter, section.
s.m. An owl; (met.) a stupid fellow, blockhead, fool; (in gamblers' parlance) one to be rooked, a 'pigeon'; — adj. Stupid, foolish, senseless
subs: v.t. To make a fool of, to befool; to take in, gull, cheat
ullū honā, v.n. To become a fool, make a fool of oneself, stultify oneself; to be intoxicated
v.t. To entrap a green fellow, catch a gull, rook a pigeon
s.m. lit. 'A young owl'; an arrant fool, a great idiot
ullū-dāsrī, s.m. A great simpleton, fool, idiot
lit. 'To eat the flesh of an owl'; to become foolish, stupid, or idiotic.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀉𑀮𑀽𑀅 (ulūa), from Sanskrit उलूक (úlūka). Cognate with Gujarati ઉલ્લૂ (ullū) and Punjabi الو (ullū).

def: s.m. A kind of creeper; a kind of reed or grass, Saccharum cylindricum.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀉𑀮𑀽𑀅 (ulūa), from Sanskrit उलूक (úlūka). Cognate with Gujarati ઉલ્લૂ (ullū) and Punjabi الو (ullū).

def: s.m. Owlishness, folly, stupidity.
s.m. Firebrand; burning wood; charcoal.
s.m. Firebrand; burning wood; charcoal.
s.m. A certain stroke in sword-play, a reflex or inverted blow.

adj. (f. -ī, obl. e), Reversed, turned back; inverted, head-downwards, upside-down, topsy-turvy; reverse, perverse; contrary, opposite; stupid; wrong; — s.m. Reverse, opposite, contrary; — pease-pudding (a kind of pudding made of the meal of any pulse); — adv. On the contrary, on the other hand; in spite of, notwithstanding; causelessly, unjustly
subs: v.n. To run or flee back, to turn and run away
s.m. The cut of an angarkhā to the left side, as worn by Hindūs (opp. to sīdhā parda)

adj. (f. -ī), Inverted, subverted, turned upside down or inside out; confused, disordered, deranged, topsy-turvy, higgledy-piggledy, jumbled, tangled, involved; — ulṭā-pulṭā karnā, v.t. To turn upside down, turn inside out, throw into confusion, disarrange, subvert; to disperse, scatter; to turn over and over again, to revolve (see ulaṭ-pulaṭ karnā and ulaṭnā pulaṭnā)
v.n. To go back, return, retrace one's steps
adj. Lit. 'an inverted frying-pan'; coal-black, very black
v.n. To become perversely annoyed, or unreasonably exasperated
v.n. To be born feet-foremost (a child)
v.n. To counteract; to bring a cross suit or counter action
s.m. A perverse, or crooked, or impertinent answer; retort; — ulṭā jawāb denā, v.t. To answer back; to retort; to meet (one) with impertinence
v.t. To understand amiss, misunderstand, misconceive, mistake; to pervert
(-par), To mount (a horse) the wrong way, to mount from behind
adj. (In Bot.) Bending in and out, sinuated
v.n. To turn back, retrace (one's) steps; to return (from a place) immediately after arriving
(or pair) ćalnā, v.n. To walk backwards
v.t. To misteach; to mislead; to set against, poison the mind (of)
s.f. Feigned, inconsistent, or incorrect discourse
s.m. Wrong courses; bad habits; improper conduct
v.n. To retrace (one's) steps, &c. = ulṭe pāʼon phir-jānā, q.v.
prov. The thief turns round and threatens the kotwāl; the thief turns the tables on the kotwāl
v.t. To lay on the thatch the wrong way; (met.) to criminate unjustly
s.f. A kind of coating for the huqqa tube
v.n. To breathe inwardly; to breathe imperceptibly; to gasp, &c. ( = ulṭe sāṅs lenā, q.v.)
s.f. Wrong course, improper or unlawful manner or way (cf. ulṭe ćāl); peculiar or uncertain temper, caprice
v.n. To breathe convulsively, to gasp
s.f. Misunderstanding, misapprehension; erroneous view or opinion; fallacy; perverse idea; defective or weak understanding
v.t. To scold roundly, revile, vilify, abuse, insult
v.t. To throw up, to vomit
s.f. Reverse blow, back stroke; (cf. ulpaṭ)
To count (one's) beads backwards; to invoke a curse (upon)
adj. Of an ignorant or undiscerning race or people; — perversely misconstruing or misunderstanding plain facts or straightforward matters

def: v.t. = ulaṭnā, q.v.
s.m. Overturning, upsetting, turning upside down; inversion, subversion, &c. (see ulaṭnā).
adj. = ulṭā, q.v.
s.m. Translation, version (syn. tarjuma)
subs: (-kā), To make a translation (of), to translate.

def: s.f. A kind of grass = ulū and ulūʼa, q.q.v.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic أُولُو (ʔulū).

def: pron. adj. contr. from اولو ulū, q.v. (The word occurs in compounds, as uluʼl-amr, &c.)
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀉𑀮𑀽𑀅 (ulūa), from Sanskrit उलूक (úlūka). Cognate with Gujarati ઉલ્લૂ (ullū) and Punjabi الو (ullū).

ety: for A. ʻuluwwuṇ, inf. n. of علو 'to be high,' &c.
def: s.m. Highness, eminence, sublimity; an eminence, a height.

ety: pl. of ذو ẕū: acc. ulī
def: adj. Possessors, owners; possessed of, endowed with, possessing, having: (N.B. The first و in ulū is short, being merely scriptio plma; and hence the word is often written الو ulū; — it occurs, in Urdū, only in comp. e.g.)
subs: Enterprising, ambitious; daring, determined, resolute; the enterprising, the ambitious; uluʼl-azmī, s.f. Determination, resoluteness; ambition
Possessed of discernment, discerning, wise, discreet; the discerning, &c.
Possessed of science, learned; the learned.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic أُولُو (ʔulū).

def: s. pl. (of alf), Thousands.

ety: for A. علوفة, pl. of ʻalaf, q.v.
def: s.m. Food or victuals (of soldiers, &c.), rations, provisions; subsistence allowance or money, daily pay, stipend, salary, pension.
s.f. Deity, divinity, the divine essence, Godhead (see ilāhīyat).
s.f. Deity, divinity, the divine essence, Godhead (see ilāhīyat).
s.m. An owl = ullū (the com. form, q.v.).
s.m. A wooden mortar for cleaning rice, &c. ( = okhlī, q.v.); — bdellium, a gummy substance.
v.s.v. اولو ulū for all words compounded with ulū.
s.m. Axioms
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian عُلُوم (ulūm), borrowed from Arabic عُلُوم (ʕulūm), from عِلْم (ʕilm), from عَلِمَ (ʕalima).

def: s.m. = الش ulush, q.v.
s.m. An owl = ullū. (Also written urūʼā.)
s.m. A kind of creeper; a kind of reed or grass, Saccharum cylindricum.
adj. Of or relating to the higher or upper parts or regions (orig. of Arabia); high, sublime; above; celestial, heavenly.
s.f. Flax; linseed; — light, splendour; fame, reputation; tranquillity; night; an epithet of Pārvati; an interjection expressive of astonishment.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اما (ammā), from Arabic أَمَّا (ʔammā).

def: = umaṇḍnā, q.v.
s.f. = umang, q.v.
v.n. = umangnā, q.v.
s.m. Great pleasure, intense delight, rapture, ecstasy.
s.f. pl. (of ummat), Peoples, nations, sects, tribes.

ety: S. उद्+वर्तनीयं, rt. वृत्, or rt. उद्+मदनीयं
def: v.n. To rise, swell, heave, surge; to increase; to be full, to well up; to run over, overflow; to burst forth, gush out, be poured out; to pour forth; to gather thick, to flock, throng; to abound; to be affected, touched, moved
subs: v.n. To weep violently or bitterly
v.n. (intensive of umanḍnā), To burst out or forth; to sweep along, &c.

def: s.f. Excessive joy, elation, rapture, transport; exultation, intoxication (of success); pride, conceit; excessive desire, longing; ambition. (Also written umag.)
adj. Elated, transported, exultant, flushed, intoxicated; desirous; sanguine; ambitious.
v.n. To be elated or exultant; to swell with joy or pleasure; to advance with joy or exultingly; — to swell, heave, dilate, expand, fill out; to be flushed or intoxicated (with success).
s.m. pl. (of amīr), Nobles, noble-men, grandees; the nobles of an Indian Moḥammadan court collectively; s.m. sing. A noble, nobleman.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic أُمَرَاء (ʔumarāʔ).

def: s.f. Nobility.
s.m. The glomerous fig-tree, Ficus glomerata.
s.f. Intense heat without a breath of air, sultriness.
v.n. To rot, &c. = ubasnā, upasnā, q.q.v.
part. Flooded; overflowing
subs: v.n. To become flooded; to run over, to overflow.

ety: for A. عمدة, v.n. fr. عمد 'to stay, prop,' &c.
def: s.m. A stay, prop, support, pillar; confidence, reliance, trust ( = ʻimād); — a lord, master, chief, nobleman, grandee, rich man; — adj. (f. -ī), Great, noble, grand, consequential; excellent, fine, capital; sumptuous; vital, essential, material
s.f. Greatness, dignity; excellence, worth; sumptuousness.
v.t. To cause to flow or run over; to submerge; to flood

ety: fr. ʻumda, q.v., with aff. āna
def: adv. Like a noble or grandee.
s.f. A stay, prop, support, pillar ( = ʻumda, ʻimād, ʻamūd)
subs: 'Pillar of state,' a title bestowed on officers of high rank.

def: s.f. Hope, expectation; trust, dependence; (syn. ās, q.v.); hope or prospect of offspring, pregnancy
subs: adv. In hope, in expectation, in anticipation (of)
(-ko), To give hope, to lead (one) to hope, to raise expectations; to promise; to assure
(-kī), To entertain hope, to hope, expect, look for; to trust or confide (in); to depend (on)

def: s.f. Hope, hopefulness, expectation, expectancy; dependence; apprenticeship.
v.n. To move, come or advance exultingly, &c. = umangnā, q.v.
s.f. Ear of half-ripe grain (especially when roasted); stalk of green Indian corn fried.
s.m. Stranger, foreigner.

ety: inf. & v.n. of عمق 'to be deep,' &c.
def: s.m. Bottom (of a well, &c.); depth, profundity (syn. gahrāʼī).
s.f. Mother; source, origin, foundation, basis; chief part (of a thing).
adj. Hopeful, sanguine; — s.m. An expectant ( = ummedwār, which is the more common word).
s.m. pl. (of ʻāmil, q.v.), Agents; governors, rulers; collectors of revenue, &c.
s.f. People, religious sect, people of the same religion; followers; race, nation; caste; creed, religion.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اُمَّت (ummat), borrowed from Arabic أُمَّة (ʔumma).

def: adj. Of or belonging to an ummat, q.v. — s.m. A follower of a religious sect.
adj. Hoping, hopeful, expecting, expectant; — s.m. An expectant, applicant, candidate, petitioner, supplicant, suitor; dependent; probationer
subs: v.n. To hope; to be a candidate or applicant (for a post); to serve as a probationer or an apprentice.

def: adj. Not knowing how to read or write, uneducated, illiterate.
s.f. Life; life-time, period of life; age
subs: adv. During the term of natural life; for life
adj. (f. -kī), Life-long; sufficient for a lifetime
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian عمر ('umr), taken from Arabic عُمْر (ʕumr).

ety: for A. عمرة, v.n. fr. عمر 'to live, remain,' &c.
def: s.m. (orig.), 'A visiting, a visit'; a religious visit, or a pilgrimage, to the sacred places at Mecca; — (a man's) visiting, or going in to, his (newly-married) wife in the abode of her family.
corr. of A. umarā, q.v.

ety: inf. n. of عمّ 'to be common,' &c.
def: adj. Common, general, universal, &c. ( = ʻām, q.v.).
adv. Commonly, generally, vulgarly ( = ʻalaʼl-ʻumūm).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian عموما ('umūman), from Arabic عُمُومًا (ʕumūman, “generally”).

def: s.f. The condition of being a mother, maternity.
s.m. pl. (of amr), = A امورات umurāt, s.m. pl. (of umūr), Things, affairs, matters, concerns; articles, items; actions; commands.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic أُمُور (ʔumūr).

def: s.m. pl. (of umūr), = A امور umūr, s.m. pl. (of amr), Things, affairs, matters, concerns; articles, items; actions; commands.

adj. Less, deficient, wanting, minus; one less than (in which sense it is prefixed to the even decimal numerals from twenty up to a hundred, e.g. un-nīs, one less than twenty, or nineteen; un-tīs, one less than thirty, or twenty-nine, and so on)
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀅𑀡- (aṇa-), from Sanskrit अन्- (an-), from Proto-Indo-European *n̥- (“not, un-”).

def: intj. of interrogation; of anger; of reproach; of contempt.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀅𑀡- (aṇa-), from Sanskrit अन्- (an-), from Proto-Indo-European *n̥- (“not, un-”).

def: s.f. Wool; shaggy hair that may be woven; — gādar ānī ūn-koṅ baiṭhe ćare kapās, 'I bought the sheep for (its) wool, and it does nothing but eat the cotton' (a proverb used where that which is done by way of economy or profit turns out expensive or ruinous).
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀉𑀡𑁆𑀡𑀸 (uṇṇā), from Sanskrit ऊर्णा (ū́rṇā), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *Hwŕ̥HnaH, from Proto-Indo-European *h₂wĺ̥h₁neh₂ (“wool”).

def: v.n. To whimper, cry, pule; to insist, persist (as a child).
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀉𑀡𑁆𑀡𑀸 (uṇṇā), from Sanskrit ऊर्णा (ū́rṇā), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *Hwŕ̥HnaH, from Proto-Indo-European *h₂wĺ̥h₁neh₂ (“wool”).

def: (the Form. of the oblique cases plur. of the pron. wǒh), Those; them; him (by way of respect).
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀅𑀡- (aṇa-), from Sanskrit अन्- (an-), from Proto-Indo-European *n̥- (“not, un-”).

def: adj. Less, &c. = ūn; — s.m. A sword, knife, &c. worn down by grinding.
adj. Less, short, deficient, defective, minus ( = un, q.v.); young, toothless (cattle)
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀉𑀡𑁆𑀡𑀸 (uṇṇā), from Sanskrit ऊर्णा (ū́rṇā), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *Hwŕ̥HnaH, from Proto-Indo-European *h₂wĺ̥h₁neh₂ (“wool”).
